source: Vahan (MoRTH) / FADA / state EV policies — approximate, dated reference (no clean free live feed exists; registration and policy data shift constantly)

adoption.note: India's EV story is a two- and three-wheeler story: 2W and 3W together are ~90% of every EV sold, while electric cars are still a small slice. Three-wheelers (e-rickshaws) already run around one in five new sales. Shares move month to month — this is a recent reference, not a live feed.
adoption.why: The picture most people carry — EV means an electric car — is upside down for India. Cheap electric scooters and e-rickshaws are electrifying first because the running-cost saving matters most where margins are thinnest.

gridLoad.note: Illustrative. Today's ~55 lakh EVs — mostly light 2W and 3W — draw on the order of 8 TWh a year, well under 1% of India's electricity. A scenario where 30% of the ~250 million two-wheeler fleet went electric would add roughly 38 TWh a year, about 4–5 GW of average load. Assumes ~500 kWh/year per electric two-wheeler and typical usage.
gridLoad.why: EVs are still a rounding error on the grid, so the fear isn't total energy — it's when they charge. Millions plugging in at 7pm would deepen the evening peak; the same millions charging overnight would fill the belly of the duck curve instead. Managed charging, not raw capacity, is the real question.

incentivesNote: State EV policies change often, run in phases, and cap total payouts — several have lapsed or are being redrafted. Treat this as a reference of the general shape of each state's support, not a live entitlement. Always confirm on the state's own EV-policy portal before buying.
